title: HTML Fixtures, Fake Transports, Local Servers, Properties, Faults, and Mutation · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved crawl trace contains one plausible authorization, URL, robots, HTTP, decoding, parser, selector, frontier, concurrency, metadata, accessibility, privacy, export, browser, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in layered deterministic crawler verification suite and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Build minimal, representative, malformed, Unicode, oversized, dynamic, accessibility, and changed-site fixtures with exact purpose labels.
Use fake transports for deterministic units and a controlled local server for redirect, streaming, timeout, encoding, cache, and connection evidence.
Test URL resolution, idempotent canonical keys, scope monotonicity, fragment behavior, query policies, and adversarial address forms as properties.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Html Fixture Design
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug site-quality team handles case 6670 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to combine fixed HTML, fake transports, local servers, URL properties, fault injection, and mutation evidence; personal data must remain minimized and redacted; then change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Mutation Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-mutation-evidence-s00t6k" that uses case 1629 to combine fixed HTML, fake transports, local servers, URL properties, fault injection, and mutation evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Mutation Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Mutation Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Mutation Evidence, keep Mutation Evidence connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-mutation-evidence-s00t6k
# Competency: scraper-mutation-evidence.
# Case 1629: a clean package and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope.
def evidence_scraper_mutation_evidence_s00t6k(fixtures: int = 72, changed_cases: int = 36, injected_faults: int = 18, killed_mutants: int = 16, live_public_requests: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_s00t6k = "s00t6k-k6t00s-1629"
    assert evidence_variant_s00t6k
    evidence_axes_s00t6k = (
        "authorizations00t6k",
        "url1629",
        "responsek6t00s",
        "trees00t6k1629",
        "frontier1629s00t6k",
        "reportk6t00s1629",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_s00t6k)) == 6
    if fixtures < 24 or changed_cases < 12:
        return False, fixtures, "test-depth"
    if injected_faults < 8:
        return False, injected_faults, "fault-depth"
    if killed_mutants < 8:
        return False, killed_mutants, "mutation-evidence"
    if live_public_requests > 0:
        return False, live_public_requests, "nondeterministic-live-test"
    assert killed_mutants >= 8 and live_public_requests == 0
    return True, killed_mutants, "layered-crawler-verification"
